XFileCreateMediaObjectAsync

Creates and initializes the asynchronous file Xbox Media Object (XMO).

HRESULT XFileCreateMediaObjectAsync(
    HANDLE hFile,
    DWORD dwMaxPackets,
    XFileMediaObject **ppMediaObject
);

Parameters

hFile
Handle to the file. The file handle supplied must be created using the FILE_FLAG_NO_BUFFERING and FILE_FLAG_OVERLAPPED flags. See the CreateFile function for more information.
dwMaxPackets
Value that indicates the maximum number of attached packets at any given time. This value cannot be zero.
ppMediaObject
Address of a pointer to the XFileMediaObject that is created.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful, an error code otherwise.

Remarks

This XMO provides a way to do asynchronous file I/O read requests. Submit requests using the XMediaObject::Process call, then frequently (e.g. once per frame) call XFileMediaObject::DoWork to complete processing of the xmedia packets.

The position from which the XMO reads the file can be set by calling XFileMediaObject::Seek.

The title is responsible for freeing the XFileMediaObjectAsync that is created by using the Release method.

For wave files, this function will create an XWaveFileMediaObject.

Requirements

Header: Declared in DSound.h.

Import Library: Use DSound.lib.

See Also

XFileCreateMediaObject, XFileCreateMediaObjectEx, XWaveFileCreateMediaObject, XWaveFileCreateMediaObjectEx
